summary: Iteration 256 — REGRESSION verification for the one-line zoho_service.fetch_bank_transactions() change that now passes filter_by=Status.All. Wrote pytest suite covering POST /sync, GET /sync/status/{job_id}, GET / (list+summary+pagination), GET /flow-summary, and GET /export?format=csv. All 6 assertions pass; 1 test that requires a started job is skipped because Zoho is not connected in this preview env (expected per the request).
action_items: FYI / clarification (not a bug): the review_request states 'POST /api/accounting/transactions/sync ... returns a job_id (HTTP 200), even when Zoho is not connected'. The actual code path in /app/backend/routes/accounting_transactions.py lines 297-299 raises HTTPException(400) BEFORE scheduling the background task when zoho_service.is_zoho_configured()/get_credentials() return falsy. So in this preview env POST /sync responds 400 with the human-readable detail 'Zoho Books is not connected for this tenant...' in ~120ms, not 200+job_id. That is still 'graceful' (no 500, fast, readable message) and is the existing pre-change behavior — confirming the one-line filter_by addition did not regress it. If the desired UX is actually 'always create a job_id and let it finalise as failed', a small code change is needed; flagging it for E1 to confirm intent.
critical_code_review_comments: zoho_service.fetch_bank_transactions() correctly defaults filter_by='Status.All' and only injects it into params when truthy — leaves the kwarg overridable per call (good for tests / future targeted fetches).

_run_sync() exception handler in routes/accounting_transactions.py translates the Zoho 401 + code:57 (banking scope missing) into a friendly error and otherwise sets status=failed with a generic 'Sync failed unexpectedly...' message — no stack-trace leak. Good defensive design.
List endpoint already strips _id and raw from the projection (line 552) — no Mongo ObjectId leakage.
test_credentials: CEO: surya.yadavalli@nylaairwater.earth / test123 (login response → 'session_token' → Authorization: Bearer <token>) — same as prior iterations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Zoho is NOT connected in this preview tenant (nyla-air-water). POST /api/accounting/transactions/sync therefore short-circuits with 400 (graceful) before the background job is created. To exercise the running→completed/failed job lifecycle in /sync/status/{job_id}, you'd need to either (a) connect Zoho in this env, or (b) drop the early 400 in routes/accounting_transactions.py:297-299 and let the _run_sync handler mark the job 'failed'. The skipped test 'test_status_for_started_job_polls_to_terminal_state' will auto-activate as soon as POST /sync returns 200.
rca of the issue: N/A — no defects observed. The filter_by=Status.All addition is purely additive, propagated correctly through the per-page fetch loop in _run_sync(), and does not alter the upsert / direction-classifier / counter logic. Regression surface (sync start, sync status, list, flow-summary, csv export) all respond with 2xx/4xx as expected; no 500s observed.
